title: Bank Leader Apologizes After Calling Workers 'Lower Value'
subtitle: A senior banking executive faces backlash after comments about artificial intelligence and job losses.

summary.en: The CEO of Standard Chartered, Bill Winters, recently faced public criticism for his comments regarding employees. He described workers whose jobs might be replaced by artificial intelligence as 'lower value human capital.' This remark caused significant upset among staff and the public. Winters later apologized, explaining that he meant to discuss the need for training staff for new roles. The bank continues to navigate the difficult balance between using new technology and supporting its workforce.
summary.zh: 渣打銀行執行長溫特斯近期因對員工的評論面臨公眾批評。他將那些工作可能被人工智慧取代的員工形容為「價值較低的人力資本」。此言論在員工與大眾間引發嚴重不滿。溫特斯隨後道歉，解釋其本意是討論培訓員工以勝任新職位的必要性。該銀行目前仍在努力平衡新技術應用與員工保障之間的困難處境。

paragraphs.en: The leader of a major global bank has issued an apology after using language that many people found offensive. Bill Winters, the CEO of Standard Chartered, recently spoke at an investors conference about the future of his company. During his speech, he discussed how artificial intelligence, or AI, is changing the way banks operate. AI refers to computer systems that can perform tasks normally requiring human intelligence. In his speech, Winters described employees whose jobs are at risk of being replaced by this technology as 'lower value human capital.'
paragraphs.zh: 一家全球大型銀行的領導人因使用被許多人認為具有冒犯性的言詞而公開道歉。渣打銀行（Standard Chartered）執行長溫特斯（Bill Winters）近期在一場投資人會議上談及公司未來。他在演講中討論了人工智慧（AI）如何改變銀行運作方式。人工智慧是指能執行通常需要人類智慧才能完成任務的電腦系統。溫特斯在演講中，將那些工作面臨被此類技術取代風險的員工，形容為「價值較低的人力資本」。

paragraphs.en: This choice of words quickly caused a wave of negative reactions. According to BBC Business, the comments were seen by many as disrespectful to the thousands of people who work for the bank. Standard Chartered is a large organization based in the UK, employing approximately 82,000 people worldwide. A significant number of these employees work in back-office roles, which are the positions most likely to be affected by automation. Automation is the process of using machines or software to do work that people used to do.
paragraphs.zh: 這番言論迅速引發了一波負面反應。根據英國廣播公司商業新聞（BBC Business）報導，許多人認為這些評論對該銀行數以千計的員工極不尊重。渣打銀行是一家總部位於英國的大型機構，在全球擁有約八萬兩千名員工。其中有相當大比例的員工從事後勤職務，而這些職位最容易受到自動化的影響。自動化是指利用機器或軟體來執行過去由人類完成的工作流程。

paragraphs.en: Following the backlash, Winters took to LinkedIn to explain his position and offer an apology. He stated that his words had caused upset to some colleagues, and he was sorry for the way he expressed himself. He clarified that his goal was not to insult staff, but to explain how the bank plans to manage the rise of AI. BBC Business reports that the bank expects to cut about 15% of its back-office roles over the next four years. This represents roughly 7,800 positions that could disappear as the company adopts new technology.
paragraphs.zh: 在輿論壓力下，溫特斯透過領英（LinkedIn）說明立場並致歉。他表示自己的言詞讓部分同事感到不適，並對表達方式致歉。他澄清其目的並非侮辱員工，而是為了說明銀行計畫如何應對人工智慧的興起。英國廣播公司商業新聞報導指出，該銀行預計在未來四年內裁減約百分之十五的後勤職位。這意味著隨著公司採用新技術，將有約七千八百個職位可能消失。

paragraphs.en: In his follow-up messages, Winters argued that his comments were taken out of context. He explained that he believes the bank has a responsibility to help employees move from roles that are easily automated into 'higher-value' positions. He noted that for years, the company has worked to help staff gain new skills so they can find different opportunities within the organization. He insisted that he values all his colleagues highly and is committed to helping them adapt to the fast-changing nature of the banking industry.
paragraphs.zh: 在後續訊息中，溫特斯辯稱他的評論被斷章取義。他解釋稱，他認為銀行有責任協助員工從容易被自動化的職位，轉型至「價值較高」的職位。他指出，多年來公司一直致力於協助員工習得新技能，以便他們能在組織內部尋找不同機會。他堅稱自己非常重視所有同事，並致力於協助他們適應銀行業快速變遷的本質。

paragraphs.en: Despite these explanations, many people remain unconvinced. Some individuals commented on his social media posts, suggesting that the apology did not change the fact that he had used such a term in the first place. One person wrote that the remark was either a very poor choice of words or an honest belief that was spoken aloud. Another commenter noted that the CEO might now be remembered primarily for his description of employees as having 'lower value.'
paragraphs.zh: 儘管有這些解釋，許多人仍不買帳。一些人在他的社群媒體貼文下留言，認為道歉並不能改變他最初使用該詞彙的事實。有人寫道，這番言論要麼是極其糟糕的措辭，要麼就是他內心真實的想法被說了出來。另一位評論者則指出，這位執行長未來可能主要會因為將員工形容為「價值較低」而為人所知。

paragraphs.en: This situation highlights a growing tension in the corporate world. As companies like Amazon, Meta, and Microsoft continue to announce large numbers of layoffs, many workers are worried about their future. Often, these companies blame the need for AI and new technology for the job losses. For employees, the fear is not just about losing a paycheck, but about feeling that their contributions are no longer seen as important by the people at the top of the company.
paragraphs.zh: 此情況凸顯了企業界日益緊張的氛圍。隨著亞馬遜（Amazon）、Meta 與微軟（Microsoft）等公司持續宣布大規模裁員，許多勞工對未來感到擔憂。這些公司往往將裁員歸咎於人工智慧與新技術的需求。對員工而言，恐懼不僅來自於失去薪水，更在於感覺到自己的貢獻在公司高層眼中已不再重要。

paragraphs.en: To address these concerns, Standard Chartered has tried to reassure its staff. In an internal memo shared with employees, Winters told them that he understands why the media coverage is unsettling. He promised that the bank would prioritize moving employees to new roles whenever possible. A spokesperson for the bank added that they are focused on combining the best human talent with AI tools. They also mentioned that the bank is working to provide staff with 'future-ready skills,' which will help them remain employable either at the bank or elsewhere if their current jobs are eliminated.
paragraphs.zh: 為了化解這些疑慮，渣打銀行試圖安撫員工。在發給員工的內部備忘錄中，溫特斯表示他理解媒體報導為何會造成不安。他承諾銀行將盡可能優先協助員工轉調至新職位。該銀行發言人補充說明，他們致力於將最優秀的人才與人工智慧工具結合。他們並提到，銀行正努力為員工提供「未來就緒技能」，這將有助於員工在職位被裁撤後，無論是在銀行內部或外部，都能保持就業競爭力。

paragraphs.en: As the banking sector continues to change, the way leaders talk about their employees will likely remain a sensitive topic. The case of Standard Chartered serves as a reminder that while technology is changing business, the way companies treat their people during this transition is just as important as the profits they make. Whether the bank can successfully retrain its workforce or if more job losses are inevitable remains to be seen. For now, the focus is on how the company will handle the upcoming changes with what the CEO described as 'thought and care.'
paragraphs.zh: 隨著銀行業持續變革，領導人談論員工的方式很可能仍是一個敏感議題。渣打銀行的案例提醒我們，儘管技術正在改變商業模式，但企業在轉型過程中對待員工的方式，與獲利能力同樣重要。該銀行能否成功培訓員工，或是未來將不可避免地出現更多裁員，仍有待觀察。目前，焦點在於公司將如何以執行長所稱的「深思與關懷」來處理即將到來的變革。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To start doing something, often as a way to deal with a situation or communicate.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 開始做某事（常用於指採取某種行動來回應或處理問題）。
keyTerms.noteZh: 這裡不是「帶去」的意思，而是指他選擇使用 LinkedIn 這個平台來回應。文中：Following the backlash, Winters took to LinkedIn to explain his position and offer an apology.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To reduce the number or amount of something, in this case, jobs.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作「切、割」，這裡指企業裁員。文中：BBC Business reports that the bank expects to cut about 15% of its back-office roles over the next four years.

keyTerms.definitionEn: When words are quoted without the surrounding information that explains their true meaning.
keyTerms.noteZh: 指說話者的原意被忽略，只看片面字眼。文中：In his follow-up messages, Winters argued that his comments were taken out of context.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To continue to be in a particular state or condition.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作「剩下」，這裡強調狀態持續不變。文中：Despite these explanations, many people remain unconvinced.

sources.name: BBC Business
sources.title: Bank boss sorry after describing workers as 'lower value human capital'

quiz.question: According to the article, how many positions does Standard Chartered expect to eliminate due to automation over the next four years?

options.D: A small number of back-office roles

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第三段，銀行預計在未來四年內裁減約 15% 的後台職位，文中明確指出這代表大約 7,800 個職位。選項 A 是該銀行全球員工的總數，而非裁員人數。選項 C 雖提到 15%，但這是針對「後台職位」而非「全球員工總數」。選項 D 雖然描述了職位類型，但用詞模糊，且 7,800 個職位並非「小數目」。

quiz.question: What can be inferred about the employees' reaction to Bill Winters' apology?

options.A: They believe his apology was sincere and have moved on.
options.B: They are satisfied because the bank promised to prioritize retraining.
options.C: They remain skeptical about his true feelings toward the staff.
options.D: They are primarily concerned about the 15% reduction in salaries.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。文章第五段提到，儘管 Winters 進行了道歉與解釋，許多人仍然不買帳（unconvinced），甚至有人懷疑這是否是他內心的真實想法（honest belief）。這顯示員工對於他的道歉抱持懷疑態度。選項 A 與 B 與文中描述的「unconvinced」矛盾。選項 D 錯誤，文中提到的是職位流失（job losses），而非薪資削減（salaries）。

quiz.question: In the context of the article, what does the word 'unsettling' mean in the sentence: 'Winters told them that he understands why the media coverage is unsettling'?
quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 A。在此語境中，unsettling 指的是媒體對裁員與「低價值人類資本」言論的報導，讓員工感到不安、焦慮。選項 B 是字面上的「不穩定」，不符合語境。選項 C 與 D 與該詞彙的定義完全無關。該詞在商務報導中常用於形容令人感到威脅或心神不寧的資訊。

quiz.question: What is the primary message of the article regarding the rise of AI in the banking sector?

options.A: AI is the most efficient way for banks to increase their annual profits.
options.B: Standard Chartered is the only bank currently struggling with AI implementation.
options.C: The way companies communicate with employees during technological transitions is critical.
options.D: The CEO of Standard Chartered should be replaced due to his offensive comments.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。文章最後一段總結提到，雖然科技改變了商業模式，但企業在轉型期間對待員工的方式（溝通與態度）同樣重要。文章透過 Winters 的爭議事件，探討了領導者言論對員工士氣與企業文化的影響。選項 A 並非文章主旨，文章主要探討的是人與技術的衝突。選項 B 與 D 皆為過度推論或未在文中被視為核心論點。

aiDisclaimer: 本文由 AI 綜合多家報導生成，事實請以原始來源為準。
